title: Skip Connections
concept: A skip connection sends the input around one or more layers and combines it later, giving the network a direct path that preserves information even while deeper layers try to refine it.

exercises.prompt: Recap: In a very deep plain network, why can simply stacking more layers backfire?

options.text: Because optimization gets harder, so training can degrade instead of improve.
Because extra layers force the dataset to change labels.
Because deep models cannot represent complex functions.

exercises.explain: Yes. The problem is not that depth is useless. The problem is that a long plain chain becomes hard to train well.

cards.title: An express train past local stops
cards.body: If every passenger had to get off at every small station, long trips would become painfully slow. An express train fixes that by letting some passengers travel farther with fewer interruptions. A skip connection gives information that kind of express route through a deep network.

cards.title: What a skip connection does
cards.body: A skip connection lets a signal jump over one or more layers instead of forcing all information through every transformation in sequence. The skipped signal is later combined with the output of the deeper layers, so the network can preserve useful original information while still learning refinements.

cards.title: A bridge over traffic
cards.body: Picture a city route where the main road is crowded with stoplights. A bridge lets part of the traffic flow over the jam instead of getting trapped in every delay. Skip connections act like that bridge for activations and learning signals.

cards.title: Keep the base image clues alive
cards.body: Suppose early layers already found useful edges and shapes in an image. If every later layer must completely rewrite those features, important clues may get weakened. A skip connection lets the original features stay available while deeper layers add improvements on top.

cards.title: Skipping is not the same as doing nothing
cards.body: A common myth is that skip connections make the middle layers pointless. Not true. The skipped path keeps a clean copy of the earlier signal available, while the middle layers still learn valuable transformations. The network gets both preservation and refinement.

cards.title: One road vs. road plus bypass
cards.body: How information can move through a block of layers:

left.text:
Every feature must pass
through every layer
No direct shortcut

right.text:
Main path still learns
But a direct route preserves signal
and eases the trip

exercises.prompt: Which description best matches a skip connection?

options.text: A path that lets information jump around some layers and be combined later.
A rule that permanently deletes the skipped layers from the model.
A way to randomize all weights at the end of each epoch.
A training trick that changes labels into hidden activations.

exercises.explain: Correct. The key idea is a direct path around layers, not deleting layers or randomizing the model.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A skip connection means the layers it jumps over can never learn anything useful.
exercises.explain: False. Those layers still learn. The skip path just ensures the original signal can travel directly too.

exercises.sentence: A skip connection gives information a more ___ route through the network.
exercises.explain: Exactly. The point is to create a more direct route so useful information does not have to be remade at every layer.

exercises.prompt: Which architecture idea sounds most like a skip connection?

options.text: The block's input is also sent around the block and merged with its output.
Every batch permanently removes the deepest layer from training.
Each neuron chooses a different loss function on every step.

exercises.explain: Right. A skip connection reuses the block input later instead of forcing everything through only the main path.

exercises.prompt: A team is training a deep vision model. They add connections that carry early features directly around some layer groups and combine them later. What benefit are they mainly aiming for?

options.text: Preserving useful information with a direct route so deeper optimization becomes easier.
Forcing the network to stop using deeper layers at all.
Replacing gradient descent with random guessing.
Making every layer output exactly the same activation values.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Skip connections are meant to keep information and learning signals moving cleanly while the deeper layers focus on refinement.
